sections.label: Section 1.863
sections.original: εἴ μοι ξυνείη φέροντι
sections.translation: May destiny still find me winning the praise of reverent purity in all words and

sections.label: Section 1.865
sections.original: ἔργων τε πάντων , ὧν νόμοι πρόκεινται
sections.translation: deeds sanctioned by those laws of sublime range , called into life through the high clear aether , whose father is Olympus alone . Their parent was no race of mortal men ,

sections.label: Section 1.870
sections.original: ἔτικτεν οὐδὲ μή ποτε λάθα κατακοιμάσῃ ·
sections.translation: no , nor shall oblivion ever lay them to sleep : the god is mighty in them , and he does not grow old .

sections.label: Section 1.873
sections.original: ὕβρις φυτεύει τύραννον ·
sections.translation: Insolence breeds the tyrant . Insolence , once vainly stuffed with wealth

sections.label: Section 1.875
sections.original: ἃ μὴ ʼπίκαιρα μηδὲ συμφέροντα ,
sections.translation: that is not proper or good for it , when it has scaled the topmost ramparts , is hurled to a dire doom , where one ’ s feet cannot serve to good advantage . But I pray that the god never

sections.label: Section 1.880
sections.original: χρῆται . τὸ καλῶς δʼ ἔχον
sections.translation: quell such rivalry as benefits the state . I will always hold the god as our protector .

sections.label: Section 2.884
sections.original: εἰ δέ τις ὑπέροπτα χερσὶν ἢ λόγῳ πορεύεται ,
sections.translation: But if any man walks haughtily in deed or word ,

sections.label: Section 2.885
sections.original: δίκας ἀφόβητος οὐδὲ δαιμόνων ἕδη σέβων ,
sections.translation: with no fear of Justice , no reverence for the images of gods , may an evil doom seize him for his ill-starred pride , if he does not gain his advantage fairly ,

sections.label: Section 2.890
sections.original: καὶ τῶν ἀσέπτων ἔρξεται
sections.translation: or avoid unholy deeds , but seeks to lay profaning hands on sanctities . Where such things occur , what mortal shall boast any more that he can ward off the arrow of the gods from his life ?

sections.label: Section 2.895
sections.original: εἰ γὰρ αἱ τοιαίδε πράξεις τίμιαι ,
sections.translation: No . For if such deeds are held in honor , why should we join in the sacred dance ?

sections.label: Section 2.897
sections.original: οὐκέτι τὸν ἄθικτον εἶμι γᾶς ἐπʼ ὀμφαλὸν σέβων ,
sections.translation: No longer will I go reverently to the earth ’ s central and inviolate shrine , no more to Abae ’ s temple or to Olympia ,

sections.label: Section 2.900
sections.original: οὐδʼ ἐς τὸν Ἀβαῖσι ναὸν οὐδὲ τὰν Ὀλυμπίαν ,
sections.translation: if these oracles do not fit the outcome , so that all mortals shall point at them with their fingers .

sections.label: Section 2.905
sections.original: Ζεῦ , πάντʼ ἀνάσσων , μὴ λάθοι
sections.translation: No , King — if this you are rightly called — Zeus all ruling , may it not escape you and your deathless power ! The old prophecies concerning Laius are fading ; already men give them no value , and nowhere is Apollo glorified with honors ;

sections.label: Section 2.910
sections.original: ἔρρει δὲ τὰ θεῖα .
sections.translation: the worship of the gods is perishing .

sections.label: Section 911
sections.original: χώρας ἄνακτες , δόξα μοι παρεστάθη
sections.translation: Princes of the land , I am planning to visit the shrines of the gods , with this wreathed branch and these gifts of incense in my hands . For Oedipus excites his soul excessively with all sorts of grief ,

sections.label: Section 915
sections.original: λύπαισι παντοίαισιν · οὐδʼ ὁποῖʼ ἀνὴρ
sections.translation: as he does not judge the new things from the old , like a man of sense , but is under the control of the speaker , if he speaks of frightful things . Since , then , I can do no good by counsel , to you , Lycean Apollo — for you are nearest —

sections.label: Section 920
sections.original: ἱκέτις ἀφῖγμαι τοῖσδε σὺν κατεύγμασιν ,
sections.translation: I have come as a suppliant with these symbols of prayer , that you may find us some escape from uncleanliness . For now we are all afraid , like those who see fear in the helmsman of their ship .

sections.label: Section 924
sections.original: ἆρʼ ἂν παρʼ ὑμῶν , ὦ ξένοι , μάθοιμʼ ὅπου
sections.translation: Can you tell me , strangers , where

sections.label: Section 925
sections.original: τὰ τοῦ τυράννου δώματʼ ἐστὶν Οἰδίπου ;
sections.translation: the house of King Oedipus is ? Or better still , tell me where he himself is , if you know .

sections.label: Section 927
sections.original: στέγαι μὲν αἵδε , καὐτὸς ἔνδον , ὦ ξένε ·
sections.translation: This is his dwelling , and he himself , stranger , is within . This lady here is the mother of his children .

sections.label: Section 929
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ὀλβία τε καὶ ξὺν ὀλβίοις ἀεὶ
sections.translation: Then may she be ever happy in a happy home ,

sections.label: Section 930
sections.original: γένοιτʼ , ἐκείνου γʼ οὖσα παντελὴς δάμαρ .
sections.translation: since she is his blessed queen .

sections.label: Section 931
sections.original: αὔτως δὲ καὶ σύ γʼ , ὦ ξένʼ · ἄξιος γὰρ εἶ
sections.translation: Happiness to you also , stranger ! Your fair greeting deserves this . But say what you have come to seek or to tell .

sections.label: Section 934
sections.original: ἀγαθὰ δόμοις τε καὶ πόσει τῷ σῷ , γύναι .
sections.translation: Good tidings , lady , for your house and your husband .

sections.label: Section 935
sections.original: τὰ ποῖα ταῦτα ; παρὰ τίνος δʼ ἀφιγμένος ;
sections.translation: What are they ? From whom have you come ?

sections.label: Section 936
sections.original: ἐκ τῆς Κορίνθου · τὸ δʼ ἔπος οὑξερῶ τάχα ,
sections.translation: From Corinth , and at the message I will give now you will doubtless rejoice , though you may perhaps grieve too .

sections.label: Section 938
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστι ; ποίαν δύναμιν ὧδʼ ἔχει διπλῆν ;
sections.translation: What is it ? Why has it this double potency ?

sections.label: Section 939
sections.original: τύραννον αὐτὸν οὑπιχώριοι χθονὸς
sections.translation: The people will make him king of the

sections.label: Section 940
sections.original: τῆς Ἰσθμίας στήσουσιν , ὡς ηὐδᾶτʼ ἐκεῖ .
sections.translation: Isthmian land , as it was said there .

sections.label: Section 941
sections.original: τί δʼ ; οὐχ ὁ πρέσβυς Πόλυβος ἐγκρατὴς ἔτι ;
sections.translation: How then ? Is the aged Polybus no longer in power ?

sections.label: Section 942
sections.original: οὐ δῆτʼ , ἐπεί νιν θάνατος ἐν τάφοις ἔχει .
sections.translation: No . For death holds him in the tomb .

sections.label: Section 943
sections.original: πῶς εἶπας ; ἦ τέθνηκε Πόλυβος , ὦ γέρον ;
sections.translation: What do you mean ? Is Polybus dead , old man ?

sections.label: Section 944
sections.original: εἰ μὴ λέγω τἀληθές , ἀξιῶ θανεῖν .
sections.translation: If I do not speak the truth , I am content to die .

sections.label: Section 945
sections.original: ὦ πρόσπολʼ , οὐχὶ δεσπότῃ τάδʼ ὡς τάχος
sections.translation: Handmaid , away with all speed , and tell this to your master ! Oracles of the gods , where do you stand now ? It is this man that Oedipus long feared he would slay . And now this man has died in the course of destiny , not by his hand .

sections.label: Section 950
sections.original: ὦ φίλτατον γυναικὸς Ἰοκάστης κάρα ,
sections.translation: Iocasta , dearest wife , why have you summoned me forth from these doors ?

sections.label: Section 952
sections.original: ἄκουε τἀνδρὸς τοῦδε , καὶ σκόπει κλύων
sections.translation: Hear this man , and judge , as you listen , what the awful oracles have come to .

sections.label: Section 954
sections.original: οὗτος δὲ τίς ποτʼ ἐστὶ καὶ τί μοι λέγει ;
sections.translation: Who is he and what news does he have for me ?

sections.label: Section 955
sections.original: ἐκ τῆς Κορίνθου , πατέρα τὸν σὸν ἀγγελῶν
sections.translation: He comes from Corinth to tell you that your father Polybus lives no longer , but has perished .

sections.label: Section 957
sections.original: τί φῄς , ξένʼ ; αὐτός μοι σὺ σημάντωρ γενοῦ .
sections.translation: How , stranger ? Let me have it from your own mouth .

sections.label: Section 958
sections.original: εἰ τοῦτο πρῶτον δεῖ μʼ ἀπαγγεῖλαι σαφῶς ,
sections.translation: If I must first make these tidings plain , know indeed that he is dead and gone .

sections.label: Section 960
sections.original: πότερα δόλοισιν ἢ νόσου ξυναλλαγῇ ;
sections.translation: By treachery , or from illness ?

sections.label: Section 961
sections.original: σμικρὰ παλαιὰ σώματʼ εὐνάζει ῥοπή .
sections.translation: A light tilt of the scale brings the aged to their rest .

sections.label: Section 962
sections.original: νόσοις ὁ τλήμων , ὡς ἔοικεν , ἔφθιτο .
sections.translation: Ah , he died , it seems , of sickness ?

sections.label: Section 963
sections.original: καὶ τῷ μακρῷ γε συμμετρούμενος χρόνῳ .
sections.translation: Yes , and of the long years that he had lived .

sections.label: Section 964
sections.original: φεῦ φεῦ , τί δῆτʼ ἄν , ὦ γύναι , σκοποῖτό τι
sections.translation: Alas , alas ! Why indeed , my wife , should one look to the

sections.label: Section 965
sections.original: τὴν Πυθόμαντιν ἑστίαν ἢ τοὺς ἄνω
sections.translation: hearth of the Pythian seer , or to the birds that scream above our heads , who declared that I was doomed to slay my father ? But he is dead , and lies beneath the earth , and here I am , not having put my hand to any spear — unless , perhaps , he died out of longing for me :

sections.label: Section 970
sections.original: κατέφθιθʼ · οὕτω δʼ ἂν θανὼν εἴη ʼξ ἐμοῦ .
sections.translation: thus , indeed , I would be the cause of his death . But as the oracles stand , at least , Polybus has swept them with him to his rest in Hades . They are worth nothing .

sections.label: Section 973
sections.original: οὔκουν ἐγώ σοι ταῦτα προύλεγον πάλαι ;
sections.translation: Did I not long ago foretell this to you ?

sections.label: Section 974
sections.original: ηὔδας · ἐγὼ δὲ τῷ φόβῳ παρηγόμην .
sections.translation: You did , but I was mislead by my fear .

sections.label: Section 975
sections.original: μὴ νῦν ἔτʼ αὐτῶν μηδὲν ἐς θυμὸν βάλῃς .
sections.translation: Now no longer take any of those things to heart .

sections.label: Section 976
sections.original: καὶ πῶς τὸ μητρὸς οὐκ ὀκνεῖν λέχος με δεῖ ;
sections.translation: But surely I must fear my mother ’ s bed .

sections.label: Section 977
sections.original: τί δʼ ἂν φοβοῖτʼ ἄνθρωπος ᾧ τὰ τῆς τύχης
sections.translation: What should a mortal man fear , for whom the decrees of Fortune are supreme , and who has clear foresight of nothing ? It is best to live at random , as one may .

sections.label: Section 980
sections.original: σὺ δʼ εἰς τὰ μητρὸς μὴ φοβοῦ νυμφεύματα ·
sections.translation: But fear not that you will wed your mother . Many men before now have slept with their mothers in dreams . But he to whom these things are as though nothing bears his life most easily .

sections.label: Section 984
sections.original: καλῶς ἅπαντα ταῦτʼ ἂν ἐξείρητό σοι ,
sections.translation: All these words of yours would have been well said ,

sections.label: Section 985
sections.original: εἰ μὴ ʼκύρει ζῶσʼ ἡ τεκοῦσα · νῦν δʼ ἐπεὶ
sections.translation: were my mother not alive . But as it is , since she lives , I must necessarily fear , though you do speak well .

sections.label: Section 987
sections.original: καὶ μὴν μέγας γʼ ὀφθαλμὸς οἱ πατρὸς τάφοι .
sections.translation: Your father ’ s death is a great sign for us to take cheer .

sections.label: Section 988
sections.original: μέγας , ξυνίημʼ · ἀλλὰ τῆς ζώσης φόβος .
sections.translation: Great , I know . But my fear is of her who lives .

sections.label: Section 989
sections.original: ποίας δὲ καὶ γυναικὸς ἐκφοβεῖσθʼ ὕπερ ;
sections.translation: And who is the woman about whom you fear ?

sections.label: Section 990
sections.original: Μερόπης , γεραιέ , Πόλυβος ἧς ᾤκει μέτα .
sections.translation: Merope , old man , the consort of Polybus .

sections.label: Section 991
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστʼ ἐκείνης ὑμὶν ἐς φόβον φέρον ;
sections.translation: And what is it in her that moves your fear ?
